raw_consumer_statement:
I had a reoccuring charge for XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX go to my Amex account. When I got the new charges on XX/XX/year>. I immediately cancelled both accounts but they did not come off my AMEX statement. 

The next step was to open a dispute. Which I did. The dispute for XXXX was resolved and the charges were reversed. But when Amex reached out to XXXX XXXX said they told me how to close the account ( via the chat thread which AMEX sent me ) but they claim, I did not follow the steps. 

Amex asked me to write a letter by XX/XX/year> which I did. The letter stated " To whom it may concern : When I first saw the charge on my Amex account in XX/XX/year>, I closed the account the next day as per the instructions in the chat message sent. The chat said that since it was within 3 days of the charge, I would receive an immediate refund. 
In XXXX, Amex rebilled my account. I then tried to access the XXXX website to ensure the account was closed. When I tried to login with my XXXX, email and password, I received the error message attached that my account was closed. 
I do not feel like I should pay for a service, I can not use, I followed their instructions to close the account. '' Login message I received today for email XXXX and it is the same message I received on XX/XX/year> - " Your account is cancelled. Please contact us to reactivate '' Why should I pay a 1 year subscription for a cancelled account that I have not used and cancelled 5 months and 8 days ago
